inline_review:
commit 2a0dfb5161686e260c5cb95123d6a77c7e5c8da8
Author: Shivam Kalra <shivamkalra98@zohomail.in>
lib/test_vmalloc: add vrealloc test case

This commit introduces a new test case "vrealloc_test" to validate the
shrink and in-place grow paths of vrealloc().

> diff --git a/lib/test_vmalloc.c b/lib/test_vmalloc.c
> index 876c72c18a0c..ce2b2777a785 100644
> --- a/lib/test_vmalloc.c
> +++ b/lib/test_vmalloc.c
[ ... ]
> @@ -421,6 +422,56 @@ vm_map_ram_test(void)
>  	return nr_allocated != map_nr_pages;
>  }
>  
> +static int vrealloc_test(void)
> +{
> +	void *ptr;
> +	int i;
> +
> +	for (i = 0; i < test_loop_count; i++) {
> +		ptr = vmalloc(PAGE_SIZE);
> +		if (!ptr)
> +			return -1;
> +
> +		*((__u8 *)ptr) = 'a';
> +
> +		/* Grow: beyond allocated pages, triggers full realloc. */
> +		ptr = vrealloc(ptr, 4 * PAGE_SIZE, GFP_KERNEL);

Does this code leak the original memory block when vrealloc() fails?

If vrealloc() returns NULL, the ptr variable is overwritten, losing the
reference to the originally allocated memory block. Could a temporary
pointer be used to preserve the original reference so it can be freed?

> +		if (!ptr)
> +			return -1;
> +
> +		if (*((__u8 *)ptr) != 'a')
> +			return -1;

Does this return path leak the memory block referenced by ptr? 

It looks like the function returns -1 directly without calling vfree(ptr)
to clean up the allocation. This same pattern appears to be repeated in the
subsequent shrink and grow steps below.

[ ... ]
